2605.23491.title: CoSPlay: Cooperative Self-Play at Test-Time with Self-Generated Code and Unit Test
2605.23491.paper_summary: Recently, Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) and Test-Time Scaling (TTS) have advanced LLM code generation through executable verification. Yet Ground-Truth Unit Tests (GT UTs) remain a bottleneck: SOTA RLVR methods require them for costly training, while existing TTS methods lose competitiveness without them. This motivates GT-free TTS, where existing methods directly use self-generated UTs to refine and select code candidates. Yet such UTs are often noisy or spuriously coupled with wrong code, and UT quality in turn cannot be validated without reliable code. The key challenge is therefore to jointly improve both. To this end, we present CoSPlay, a GT-free, training-free framework that jointly improves codes and UTs through cooperative self-play. It first explores diverse solution ideas and identifies their potential failure modes to produce discriminative UT ideas. It then uses bidirectional pass-count signals from the Code-UT execution matrix to iteratively prune or fix weak codes and refresh or replace unreliable UTs, letting the two pools co-evolve. Finally, when multiple codes remain tied at the highest pass count, it picks the final code from the largest output-consensus cluster, since correct codes agree on the same inputs while wrong codes diverge. Experiments on four challenging benchmarks show that CoSPlay on Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct improves average BoN from 22.1% to 33.2% and UT accuracy from 14.6% to 78.3%, matching or surpassing the RLVR model CURE-7B. When applied to CURE-7B, it further improves BoN by 5.7%. CoSPlay also generalizes across diverse backbones and outperforms GT-free TTS baselines under comparable token budgets, with continued gains as the budget scales up. These results suggest a scalable inference strategy for competitive code generation without any GT data.
2605.23491.paper_summary_zh: 最近，具有可驗證獎勵的強化學習（RLVR）和測試時擴展（TTS）通過可執行驗證推進了 LLM 代碼生成。然而，真實單元測試（GT UTs）仍然是一個瓶頸：最先進的 RLVR 方法需要它們進行昂貴的訓練，而現有的 TTS 方法在沒有它們的情況下失去競爭力。這促使了無 GT 的 TTS，其中現有方法直接使用自生成的 UT 來精煉和選擇代碼候選者。然而，這種 UT 通常是嘈雜的或與錯誤代碼虛假耦合，並且 UT 的質量反過來又無法在沒有可靠代碼的情況下進行驗證。因此，關鍵挑戰是共同改進兩者。為此，我們提出了 CoSPlay，一個無 GT、無需訓練的框架，通過合作自我遊戲共同改進代碼和 UT。它首先探索多樣的解決方案思路，並識別其潛在的失敗模式，以產生具辨別性的 UT 思路。然後，它利用來自代碼-UT 執行矩陣的雙向通過計數信號，迭代修剪或修復弱代碼，並刷新或替換不可靠的 UT，使這兩個池共同演化。最後，當多個代碼在最高通過計數上仍然平手時，它從最大輸出共識集群中選擇最終代碼，因為正確的代碼在相同的輸入上達成一致，而錯誤的代碼則會發散。在四個具有挑戰性的基準測試中的實驗顯示，CoSPlay 在 Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct 上將平均 BoN 從 22.1% 提高到 33.2%，UT 準確率從 14.6% 提高到 78.3%，與 RLVR 模型 CURE-7B 相匹配或超越。當應用於 CURE-7B 時，它進一步將 BoN 提高了 5.7%。CoSPlay 也在多樣的基礎架構上具有良好的泛化能力，並在可比較的標記預算下超越無 GT 的 TTS 基準，隨著預算的增加，持續獲得增益。這些結果表明了一種可擴展的推理策略，用於在沒有任何 GT 數據的情況下進行競爭性的代碼生成。
2605.23491.author: Zhangyi Hu et.al.
2605.23491.authors: Zhangyi Hu, Chenhui Liu, Tian Huang, Jindong Li, Yang Yang, Jiemin Wu, Zining Zhong, Menglin Yang, Yutao Yue
